title: Endoscopic Modified Lothrop procedure for recurrent frontal sinus disease
author: Garba Mainasara Mohammed
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Abstract:    Background   
  Endoscopic modified Lothrop has been a procedure of choice especially in recurrent frontal sinus disease but must centers in Sub Saharan Africa still practice open approach to manage frontal sinus disease.  
  Objective   
  To show the long-term outcome of endoscopic modified Lothrop procedure.  
  Methods   
  Review of patients that underwent endoscopic modified Lothrop in two institutions by single surgeon from January 2013 to December 2023. Patients’ demographic data, symptoms, indications and outcome were analyzed.   
  Results   
  Thirty-two patients underwent Endoscopic Modified Lothrop with an average age of 41.2 years, ninety two percent have favorable outcome, three patients were lost to follow up  
  Conclusions  
  Endoscopic Modified Lothrop procedure gives better outcome with less morbidity better cosmesis and less hospital stay, no recurrence as compared to an open external approach.  
   Keywords:    Recurrent, frontal sinus, Endoscopic, Modified Lothrop
